prose:
Fukui indices (f+, f-, dual descriptor) for pyridine were computed using Density Functional Theory (DFT) with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set. This corresponds to the `standard` tier in chemkit's presets. The calculation was performed in the gas phase with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1 for the neutral parent molecule. The cation state (for f+) had a charge of 1 and multiplicity 2, and the anion state (for f-) had a charge of -1 and multiplicity 2. The geometry was taken from the user-supplied `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz` file. The calculations were performed using the PySCF quantum chemistry package. All SCF calculations converged.

The results are as follows (atom index is 0-based, atom label is 1-based from the input XYZ file):

| Atom (0-based Index) | Symbol | f+ (Electrophilicity) | f- (Nucleophilicity) | Dual Descriptor (f2) |
| :------------------- | :----- | :-------------------- | :------------------- | :------------------- |
| 0 (1)                | N      | 0.4842                | 0.0641               | 0.4201               |
| 1 (2)                | C      | 0.3245                | 0.0062               | 0.3183               |
| 2 (3)                | C      | 0.1403                | 0.1182               | 0.0221               |
| 3 (4)                | C      | 0.1404                | 0.1183               | 0.0221               |
| 4 (5)                | C      | 0.2996                | 0.1121               | 0.1876               |
| 5 (6)                | C      | 0.2995                | 0.1120               | 0.1875               |
| 6 (7)                | H      | -0.1415               | 0.0828               | -0.2244              |
| 7 (8)                | H      | -0.1337               | 0.0982               | -0.2319              |
| 8 (9)                | H      | -0.1337               | 0.0982               | -0.2318              |
| 9 (10)               | H      | -0.1398               | 0.0950               | -0.2348              |
| 10 (11)              | H      | -0.1398               | 0.0949               | -0.2348              |

The most electrophilic site is Atom 0 (Nitrogen), with an f+ value of 0.4842.
The most nucleophilic site is Atom 3 (Carbon), with an f- value of 0.1183.

The calculation was trustworthy according to the engine's integrity check. The output JSON is available at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpd1hh9ivm/live.json`, and the live `.out` log file for the calculation would have been generated in the same directory.
